title: 10 best herbal teas for coughing fits

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like terpenes and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, relieving congestion and discomfort associated with coughing fits.

Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: The tea's antiviral and antimicrobial properties may help combat the underlying viral or bacterial infection causing the cough, reducing its severity and duration.

Expectorant and demulcent properties: Purple coneflower tea is rich in expectorants like mucilage, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel during a coughing fit.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, teas helps with coughing fits because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The tea has been traditionally used to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of respiratory infections, such as the common cold and flu. The active compounds in Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, work to soothe the throat, reduce inflammation, and calm coughs, providing relief from persistent and debilitating coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides (specifically, echinacoside and caftaric acid glycoside): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and swelling in the throat associated with coughing fits.

Polyphenols (specifically, caffeic acid and chicoric acid): These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the throat, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing fits.

Isoflavonoids (specifically, echinacoside): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help boost the body's immune response and reduce the severity of coughing fits caused by infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory system.
Leaves: The leaves contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and combat infection in the lungs and airways, making them a common ingredient in cough teas.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their antiviral and antiseptic properties, which help to alleviate coughs and colds by reducing the severity of respiratory infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in a quantity of 2 teaspoons fresh or 1 teaspoon dried.
Combine echinacea purpurea with 8 ounces boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes or until the liquid has cooled to a comfortable temperature.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Serve the echinacea purpurea tea hot or iced and enjoy as desired.

remedy_properties: Antitussive compounds: The tea contains antitussive compounds like ginkgolides and bilobalide, which help to suppress the cough reflex and reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits.

Inflammation-reducing properties: Maidenhair Tree tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate inflammation in the respiratory tract, reducing swelling and irritation that may be contributing to coughing fits.
Expectorant action: The tea's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and coughing fits.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with coughing fits because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in the tea work to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways. This helps to relieve coughing and provide quick relief from persistent coughs, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. It also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These help with coughing fits by reducing inflammation in the airways and improving blood flow to the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Bilobalide: This terpene derivative has anti-inflammatory properties that help calm down the airway inflammation and reduce the severity of coughing fits.
Quercetin: As a powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, quercetin helps to reduce the swelling and irritation in the airways, thereby easing coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used to make teas, as they contain a compound called bilobalide, which helps to reduce coughing and other respiratory issues.

Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are sometimes used to make teas, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted herbal supplier or harvest your own leaves.
Steep the dried leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their active compounds slowly.
Strain the liquid using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solid particles and leaves completely.
Discard the solids and let the liquid cool to room temperature for 10-15 minutes to enhance flavor and aroma.
Serve the ginkgo biloba tea in a cup and enjoy it as a warm or cold beverage at any time of day.

remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has antibacterial properties that help combat infection-causing bacteria in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and alleviating coughs.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in thyme, such as camphor and borneol, have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to ease congestion and soothe a sore throat, which can contribute to coughing fits.

Antioxidants: Thyme tea is rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the severity of coughing fits caused by environmental pollutants or other irritants.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, teas helps with coughing fits because it contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce the severity and frequency of coughs by soothing the throat and alleviating congestion. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties aid in loosening and removing mucus from the airways, providing relief from coughing fits and promoting easier breathing. This makes thyme tea a popular remedy for respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol helps with coughing fits by reducing inflammation and acting as a natural expectorant to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, helping to relax airway muscles and ease coughing fits.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated airways to alleviate coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part to make teas for coughing fits, as they contain thymol, a compound known for its expectorant and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make teas for coughing fits, as they have a soothing effect on the throat and provide relief from coughing and congestion.
Buds: Buds are used to make teas for coughing fits, as they contain essential oils that help to ease coughs and promote respiratory health.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers per 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried herb in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Add honey or sugar to taste if desired to sweeten the tea.
Serve the tea hot or iced depending on your preference and enjoy immediately.

remedy_properties: Antiviral and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender tea's antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can ease congestion and soothe a cough.

Expectorant Properties: The expectorant properties of lavender tea help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.

Calming and Sedative Effects: Lavender tea's calming and sedative effects can help relax the throat and respiratory muscles, reducing spasms and discomfort that can trigger or exacerbate coughing fits.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, teas helps with coughing fits because of its natural antispasmodic and expectorant properties. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to relax the airways and reduce inflammation, making it easier to cough up mucus and phlegm. This can provide quick relief from persistent coughs and congestion associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and allergies. As a result, Lavandula angustifolia tea has become a popular natural remedy for soothing coughs and promoting a peaceful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene found in Lavandula angustifolia, linalool has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and calm the muscles in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating coughing fits.
Lavandulol: Another terpene present in the plant, lavandulol has expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Linalyl acetate: A terpene ester in Lavandula angustifolia, linalyl acetate has antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax the muscles in the airways and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating coughing fits and promoting easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe the throat and reduce coughing.
Flowers: Utilized for their antispasmodic and antiseptic properties to calm the respiratory system and alleviate coughing fits.
Buds: Employed for their expectorant properties to help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and store them in an airtight container.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add honey to taste and stir the tea to dissolve the sweetener completely within 30 seconds.
Enjoy the tea immediately and discard the tea leaves or flowers after use.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm tea helps relax the muscles in the throat and airways, reducing spasms and coughing fits by calming down irritated tissues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in lemon balm tea have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to coughs and congestion.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of melissa leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content and freshness.
Dry the harvested leaves in a dehydrator at 95°f for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried melissa leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release flavors.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose herb particles.
Store the herbal tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of dried eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Dry the harvested botanicals for 2 hours at 150 degrees fahrenheit to prevent moisture retention.
Combine the dried eupatorium perfoliatum with 1 liter of boiling water to create a tea infusion.
Steep the tea for 10 minutes and then strain it using a fine-mesh filter to remove solids.
Store the cooled tea in a glass container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the matricaria chamomilla flowers by cutting them off when they are in full bloom.

Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve to remove the dried flowers from the liquid.

Strain the taraxacum officinale tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve to remove all solids.
Serve the taraxacum officinale tea hot or chilled depending on personal preference within 10 minutes of preparation.

Measure one teaspoon of the herb for every eight ounces of boiling water required for preparation time.
Combine the measured herb with boiling water and allow it to steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining solids before serving time.
Serve the herbal tea hot, adding honey if desired, and consuming it within a short period.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with coughing fits because it contains natural sedatives that calm the nervous system, reducing irritation in the throat and lungs. The tea's expectorant properties help loosen mucus, making it easier to expel, while its soothing effects alleviate cough spasms and congestion. Additionally, valerian's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and redness in the respiratory tract, providing fast and effective relief from persistent coughing fits. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to combat respiratory issues, offering a gentle and non-invasive way to find relief.
remedy_properties: Sedative and antispasmodic compounds: Valerian tea's sedative and antispasmodic compounds help to calm the bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation in the airways, which can help relieve coughing fits by reducing spasms and irritation.
Expectorant properties: Valerian tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughing.

Antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds: Valerian tea's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to chronic coughs and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are used because they contain valerenic acid, a compound that helps to calm coughing and soothe respiratory issues.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes of valerian are used because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce congestion and alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Rinse 1-2 tablespoons of root pieces with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine rinsed root pieces with 8 oz of boiling water and let steep for 10-15 minutes to extract essential oils.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consumption, serving warm or at room temperature as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass teas helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. The citrusy flavor and aroma of lemongrass also have a soothing effect on the throat, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate coughs. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of lemongrass may help combat underlying infections that contribute to chronic coughing, providing relief and comfort to those suffering from persistent respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass tea's antibacterial properties help combat infections that cause coughs, reducing their severity and duration.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in lemongrass tea help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel, which can provide relief from coughing fits.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, which can soothe a sore throat and ease coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing congestion and soothing irritated airways.
Rhyzomes: High in antioxidants and essential oils, which help to calm coughs and reduce inflammation in the throat and chest.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning when dew is still present on the plant for optimal flavor and potency.
Rinse the harvested leaves with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris and pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves with 8 ounces of boiling filtered water in a tea infuser and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids and discard the leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking, as hot beverages may exacerbate coughing fits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with coughing fits because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound berberine in goldenseal has been shown to soothe irritated respiratory passages, reducing inflammation and congestion that can exacerbate coughs. Additionally, goldenseal's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for easier breathing and relief from persistent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine is a natural antimicrobial compound that has been shown to reduce the severity of coughs and respiratory infections by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause them.

Isoquinoline alkaloids: Goldenseal contains various isoquinoline alkaloids, including berberine, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, leading to a decrease in coughing fits.

Antitussive compounds: Goldenseal tea also contains antitussive compounds, such as berbamin, which can help soothe and calm irritated respiratory mucous membranes, reducing coughing spasms and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant contain a high concentration of berberine, a natural compound that helps to reduce inflammation and ease coughing fits.
Barks: Goldenseal barks have been traditionally used to make tea to soothe dry, irritated throats and calm coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality goldenseal root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Measure out one teaspoon of the ground goldenseal root and combine it with one cup of boiling water in a tea infuser.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the herbal properties to meld together and the flavors to blend harmoniously.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any loose particles or sediment.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, taking care to consume it within 20-30 minutes of preparation to maximize its effectiveness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with coughing fits because of its soothing properties that calm the throat and chest. The tea's glycyrrhizin content works to reduce inflammation, ease congestion, and break down excess mucus, allowing for easier breathing and a more effective cough response. Additionally, the tea's warming quality helps to relax the muscles in the throat and chest, further alleviating discomfort and providing relief from persistent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel during a cough.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and inflammation that can exacerbate coughing fits.
Saponins: Saponins have a natural antitussive effect, which means they can help suppress or reduce the frequency of coughs by reducing the sensitivity of the airways.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving coughs.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help to soothe irritated throats and calm coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the harvested glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 8 ounces of boiling water in a medium-sized pot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes or until the liquid reaches a comfortable temperature.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.
Add honey to taste if desired and enjoy immediately, with preparation time taking approximately 15-20 minutes total.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn teas helps with coughing fits because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbs used in the tea, such as slippery elm and licorice root, work together to calm the respiratory tract and reduce inflammation caused by a cough. As the tea is consumed, it helps to loosen and clear out mucus, providing relief from congestion and coughing. Additionally, the antioxidants present in corn teas may also help to combat any underlying infections that could be contributing to the cough.
remedy_properties: Soothing mucilage: Corn tea contains a thick, protective substance called mucilage that helps to calm irritated throats and reduce inflammation, providing relief from coughs and congestion.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Corn tea is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds like polyphenols and flavonoids, which help to reduce swelling and ease respiratory discomfort caused by coughing fits.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in corn tea can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel phlegm and reducing the frequency of coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in soothing sore throats and relieving coughs.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes contain mucilages and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and ease coughing spasms.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried corn silk flowers from mature corn plants for 100g of herbal remedy.
Combine 50g of dried corn silk flowers with 200ml of boiling water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup to remove loose particles.
Add 20ml of honey syrup prepared by dissolving 1 part honey in 2 parts boiled water to sweeten tea.
Let the tea cool down to room temperature before refrigerating for up to 24 hours.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to coughing fits that are treated with herbal teas are bronchitis, asthma, and sinus infections. Herbs like ginger, peppermint, and eucalyptus help to loosen mucus, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion. These herbs can be consumed as hot or cold tea infusions to provide rapid relief from symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and chest tightness. Additionally, herbal teas containing licorice root and slippery elm may also soothe irritated throats and calm the respiratory system.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for coughing fits are natural remedies made from plants and herbs that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing fits. Examples include thyme, peppermint, ginger, and eucalyptus teas, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that ease congestion and irritation. Drinking these teas can improve sleep, reduce coughing fits, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, enhancing overall quality of life and well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with coughing fits is a blend of thyme, ginger, and licorice root. Thyme contains antimicrobial properties that combat infection, while ginger soothes the throat and reduces inflammation. Licorice root provides a protective coating to the mucous membranes, alleviating coughing. This trio works together to calm the respiratory system, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits, and promoting overall respiratory health. Drinking these teas regularly can provide relief and support the body's natural healing process.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by PloS one, herbal teas are good for coughing fits because they are a common component of East Asian traditional medicine (EATM) interventions, which show positive outcomes in most studies targeting patients with chronic cough. The study, which included 474 studies on EATM for chronic cough, found that herbal medicine was used in 80.6% of the studies and was the most common form of EATM treatment, with a total effective rate of 94.3% and cough severity assessment in 53.8% of the studies. This suggests that herbal teas, as part of EATM, can be an effective treatment option for patients experiencing cough despite conventional treatment, particularly when used for a mean of 19.1 days.
